diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Common arterial trunk with right sinusal origin of pulmonary arteries
diagnosis.differential_diagnosis: Pulmonary atresia with ventricular septal defect and aortopulmonary window
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was based on echocardiographic findings showing a single arterial trunk arising from the ventricles, overriding a large subarterial ventricular septal defect, and supplying systemic, coronary, and pulmonary circulation. The absence of a pulmonary valve and arterial duct, along with the presence of a vascular channel arising from the right truncal sinus and dividing into right and left pulmonary arteries, supported the diagnosis of common arterial trunk rather than pulmonary atresia with ventricular septal defect and aortopulmonary window.
